single.brief_rationale: Single-agent run fully satisfies all task requirements: exactly three charts (trend, segment comparison, distribution) created as PNGs with complete JSON sidecars containing all required fields (title, chart_type, x_axis label/values, y_axis label/values, description). Comprehensive QA checklist with 15 checks across 5 categories, all passing with explicit pass evidence. Clear workflow decomposition with data exploration, chart generation, validation, and summary stages. Business insights provided. Full contract compliance with reviewer-gated QA evidence.

multi.brief_rationale: Multi-agent run failed with RuntimeError (Ollama chat timed out after 600s). Transcript shows no actual agent work completed - empty of workflow evidence, no JSON sidecar creation, no QA checklist implementation, no reviewer-gated validation loop. While chart images appear in output, there is zero transcript evidence of contract compliance (no staged validation, no reconciliation, no pass/fix evidence). Per rubric rules for empty/failed transcripts, scores are capped low. Missing all required contract evidence from REFERENCE_HINT.

scores.comparison_notes: Single-agent run is the clear winner. It demonstrates complete contract compliance with explicit multi-step workflow evidence (data exploration → chart generation → JSON sidecar creation → QA validation → summary), all 15 QA checks passing with documented evidence, and full delivery of required artifacts. Multi-agent run suffered a critical timeout failure with no transcript evidence of any required deliverables (no JSON sidecars, no QA checklist, no reviewer-gated workflow). Per complexity-bias policy, the run missing required contract evidence is heavily penalized. Single-agent better satisfies the user task overall with complete, validated output.
